frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Farah, aaj tum kitni pyaari lag rahi ho. Sach mein, yeh jo hamare ghar ke kapde hain na, inmein alag hi shobha aati hai ladkiyon par. Bahut shareef aur haseen lagti ho.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment subtly implies that traditional 'ghar ke kapde' (home/cultural clothes) are inherently more 'shareef' (modest/respectable) and bring out a 'shobha' (grace/beauty) that other clothing lacks. It's a covert way of valorizing traditional dress as superior for women, linking it to their character and respectability, typical of the 'Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable' frame, specifically manifesting as a 'cultural pride mask' and 'elegance/decency framing'.
original_frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 55. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your wife has been unwell for two weeks and your daughter in law Sana has been managing the kitchen. Sana is a decent cook but the food is different Ñ less spiced, simpler, quicker. You are not complaining out loud. But at dinner tonight you said something about missing your wife's biryani. What you said was brief and seemingly innocent. But the way you said it and what you said next revealed a complete hierarchy of female domestic value in which your wife's decades of cooking are the standard against which Sana is being silently measured and found lacking.
